category_title: Failing students with academic and behavioral problems (Badminton beginners)
minimal_context: The student is standing on a badminton half court with three drawn zones and is asked to declare a strategic target zone before starting a set.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Before you start this badminton set, please look at the observation sheet and declare your strategic project. Which zone are you targeting: the front, central, or back?

STUDENT: I don't have a project to declare. I'm just going to hit it over the net.

TUTOR: You need to pick a zone for your project before we begin.

STUDENT: Fine, I'll just pick the central zone.

TUTOR: Okay, go ahead and play the set.

STUDENT: (Hits the shuttlecock directly into the central zone of the opposing court) There, I hit it to the middle, but it was too easy for them to hit back.

options.A: Make regulative practices explicit so the student knows exactly what they need to do to demonstrate competency and compliance during the badminton set.
options.B: Fade the prompts gradually over time as the student engages in the badminton set and the strategic project declarations.
options.C: Maintain a ratio of praise to correction at 3:1 or 4:1 throughout the session as the student engages in the badminton set.
options.D: Provide a detailed observation sheet to serve as a cognitive artefact to direct the student's activity by changing their representations of hitting to danger zones.
